closest_prior_works.work: PerturbHD — "Are Current AI Virtual Cell Models Useful for Scientific Discovery?" (bioRxiv 2026.04.23.719015, Arc-adjacent)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Argues abstract perturbation-prediction metrics fail to measure real utility and proposes a downstream-outcome benchmark (PerturbHD) that scores VCM predictions by their value for AI-enabled hit discovery; explicitly notes the Virtual Cell Challenge finding that rescaling predictions improves scores despite increasing error.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: PerturbCausal scores the same VCMs by utility for a DIFFERENT downstream — causal GRN/structure recovery, not hit ranking — and adds a formal invariance lemma plus a non-circular anchored ground-truth and genome-scale under-determination axis that PerturbHD does not have.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: HIGH on framing. This is the single most dangerous concurrent work: same "utility not metrics" thesis, same VCM targets, and it already flagged the rescaling artifact that is adjacent to your diagonal-rescale lemma. Arc Institute + collaborators hold all building blocks; a causal-task variant is an obvious next paper for them. Differentiate hard in the intro.

closest_prior_works.work: "When Marginals Match but Structure Fails: Covariance Fidelity in Generative Models" (arXiv 2603.17041)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Proves generically that a generative model can match all univariate marginals exactly while covariance/copula differ arbitrarily, that this covariance divergence bounds downstream regression/PCA failure (slopes can flip sign), and that controlling Frobenius covariance divergence restores stability via Davis-Kahan.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: PerturbCausal specializes this to the causal-benchmark setting: the mean-overlay evaluation channel is a positive-diagonal congruence that provably preserves the precision-support recovered by causal discovery, so it cannot reward predicted covariance. That specific causal-benchmark instantiation is not in 2603.17041.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: HIGH on the lemma's core math. The general "marginals leave covariance unconstrained, downstream flips" result is now published, so your lemma is application-only, not a new mathematical fact. Present it explicitly as a corollary of this line of work, not as a novel theorem.

closest_prior_works.work: "When Does GRN Inference Break? A Controlled Diagnostic Study of Causal and Correlational Methods on Single-Cell Data" (arXiv 2605.04930)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Uses a synthetic simulator with independent dials (dropout, latent confounders, mixing, feedback, density, sample size) to show causal methods (NOTEARS/GES) dominate only in clean regimes and collapse toward correlational baselines under pathologies; introduces an error-type decomposition.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: PerturbCausal is real-data-first and asks a question this paper explicitly names as future work: linking recovery failure to properties of REAL perturbational data, and specifically whether PREDICTED (VCM) Perturb-seq can stand in for real. It also proves the overlay-blindness result, which this diagnostic does not.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: MEDIUM. Same authors could extend to real-data / VCM-predicted inputs, but they are synthetic-only today and make no mean-blindness argument. Your real-data + prediction-substitutability axis is the exact gap they concede.

closest_prior_works.work: Ahlmann-Eltze, Huber & Anders — "Deep-learning-based gene perturbation effect prediction does not yet outperform simple linear baselines" (Nature Methods 2025) + rebuttal "...Do Outperform Uninformative Baselines on Well-Calibrated Metrics" (bioRxiv 2025.10.20)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Show foundation/DL perturbation predictors fail to beat linear/mean baselines on standard metrics; the rebuttal argues this is a metric-calibration artifact and that rank/weighted metrics reorder the models.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: PerturbCausal moves the comparison off perturbation-effect-prediction accuracy entirely and onto causal-structure recovery; its baseline-collapse story (Wall-1) is a provable property of the evaluation channel, not an empirical metric-calibration debate.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: LOW-MEDIUM. Establishes the "can't beat the mean" backdrop your Wall-1 lives in, so reviewers will ask how you differ from it. Not a direct scoop of the causal axis.

closest_prior_works.work: "Diversity by Design: Addressing Mode Collapse Improves scRNA-seq Perturbation Modeling on Well-Calibrated Metrics" (arXiv 2506.22641) + PertEval-scFM (ICML 2025)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Diagnose that single-cell perturbation generative models mode-collapse toward the mean/control, propose calibration against mean and technical-duplicate baselines, and show foundation models often fail on strong perturbations.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: PerturbCausal treats mode collapse to the mean as the MECHANISM that makes overlay-based causal evaluation blind (collapsed prediction = its per-gene mean = diagonal rescale), then proves the consequence for causal recovery rather than proposing a better generative metric.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: MEDIUM. This community owns "collapse to the mean"; if they connect collapse to a downstream causal task they would overlap your Wall-1. They currently stop at generative-metric calibration.

closest_prior_works.work: CausalBench (Chevalley et al., ICLR 2023) + CausalBench Challenge (arXiv 2308.15395; Comms Biol 2025)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Large-scale benchmark for GRN inference from REAL single-cell Perturb-seq (RPE1, K562) with interventional metrics; the challenge found interventional methods do not beat observational-only ones on real data and highlighted the synthetic-vs-real domain gap.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: CausalBench benchmarks discovery METHODS on real data; PerturbCausal benchmarks whether MODEL-PREDICTED data can replace the real data those methods consume, and proves an evaluation-channel invariance CausalBench does not address.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: MEDIUM. Same data and task family; a natural extension is "run CausalBench on VCM-predicted counts." No published version does the prediction-substitutability comparison or the lemma. This is the platform a competitor would use to scoop you.

closest_prior_works.work: STATE (Arc Institute, bioRxiv 2025.06.26) + Virtual Cell Challenge 2025; scGPT / Geneformer
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: STATE is the flagship VCM predicting perturbation response across 70+ cell lines; the Virtual Cell Challenge standardized VCM benchmarking and surfaced that simple rescaling improves scores despite worse error, and that AI models did not consistently beat statistical baselines.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: These are the systems-under-test in PerturbCausal, not competitors on your specific question. Your contribution is the causal-substitutability evaluation and lemma applied TO these models.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: HIGH institutionally. Arc holds the models, the atlas, the challenge infrastructure, and (via PerturbHD-adjacent work) the utility-benchmark framing. If Arc adds a causal-recovery track to the VCC, that is a direct scoop. Speed and a sharp causal-specific framing are your defense.

closest_prior_works.work: inspre / "Large-scale causal discovery ... in K562 cells" (Nat Commun 2025) + "Causal GRN inference from Perturb-seq via adaptive instrumental-variable modeling" (bioRxiv 2026.02.18)
closest_prior_works.what_they_did: Provide the count-aware / IV-based causal-discovery estimators (precision-support and instrument-based) for recovering GRN structure from real Perturb-seq at genome scale.
closest_prior_works.how_differs: PerturbCausal uses these as the recovery engines it feeds real vs predicted data into; the contribution is the substitutability verdict and the proof that the overlay channel neutralizes predicted covariance for exactly these precision-support estimators.
closest_prior_works.scoop_risk: LOW. These are tools you build on, not competing benchmarks; but the adaptive-IV authors could pair their method with VCM-predicted inputs, so cite and position clearly.

genuinely_new: The specific question — can a virtual-cell-model's PREDICTED Perturb-seq substitute for REAL Perturb-seq as the INPUT to causal GRN/structure recovery — with non-circular anchored ground truth and a genome-scale under-determination axis. No retrieved work runs this substitutability axis; existing benchmarks evaluate prediction accuracy (VCC, in-the-wild), hit-discovery utility (PerturbHD), or discovery-method robustness on real/synthetic data (CausalBench, When-Does-GRN-Break), none the prediction-as-input-to-causal-recovery question.

The framing of the mean-overlay evaluation channel as a positive-diagonal congruence that provably preserves the precision-support a causal-discovery method recovers, and therefore cannot reward the predicted gene-gene covariance the VCM was trained to produce. The general marginal-blindness math exists, but its instantiation as an invariance property OF the causal-benchmark evaluation channel is not stated elsewhere.

The combined two-wall honest characterization tied together in one benchmark: (Wall-1) overlay-blindness proven for the eval channel, and (Wall-2) empirical genome-scale collapse of real-data recovery into the permutation/null band — a negative-result package specific to causal substitutability.
not_new: The theorem that matching all marginals leaves the covariance/joint structure unconstrained and can flip downstream linear/regression/PCA results — now published generically (arXiv 2603.17041) and for gene-expression generative models (arXiv 2603.11244, Lotfollahi group). The math underlying your invariance lemma is established prior art, so the lemma must be presented as an application/corollary.
The empirical finding that DL/foundation perturbation predictors do not beat mean/linear baselines (Ahlmann-Eltze et al., Nature Methods 2025; PertEval-scFM; the well-calibrated-metrics rebuttal).
Mode collapse of single-cell perturbation generative models toward the mean/control (Diversity by Design, arXiv 2506.22641).

The meta-framing "abstract metrics do not measure real scientific-discovery utility, so build a downstream-outcome benchmark" — concurrent PerturbHD (bioRxiv 2026.04.23) makes exactly this argument for VCMs, and the rescaling-improves-score artifact adjacent to your diagonal-rescale point was already reported by the Virtual Cell Challenge.

The result that interventional information does not beat observational-only inference on real Perturb-seq, and that synthetic benchmarks overstate causal-method advantage (CausalBench Challenge, Comms Biol 2025).
Non-additivity of Norman double perturbations (established biology; your own gate scored the A3 non-additivity claim at 4).
sharpening: Lead with the causal-recovery substitutability axis as THE contribution and demote the invariance lemma to a corollary of the now-published covariance-fidelity results (cite arXiv 2603.17041 and 2603.11244 explicitly). Claiming the marginal-blindness math as your own theorem is the fastest way for a hostile reviewer to reject; owning it as a causal-benchmark specialization is defensible and raises perceived rigor.

Pre-empt the PerturbHD collision head-on in the intro: state that PerturbHD measures utility-for-hit-discovery while PerturbCausal measures utility-for-causal-structure, and that hit ranking and causal-edge recovery are non-substitutable downstreams. Without this paragraph reviewers will call it "PerturbHD for GRNs."

Strengthen the empirical wall so the negative result is not an overlay-harness artifact: run the non-overlay path (feed predicted CELLS directly into inspre and the adaptive-IV estimator) across at least two VCMs (STATE plus one more, e.g. scGPT/GEARS) and show recovery still sinks into the permutation band. This simultaneously answers the covariance-fidelity critique and fills the exact real-data bridge that "When Does GRN Inference Break?" concedes as future work.

Add a constructive claim so the paper is not purely negative: specify what a joint-structure-reading evaluation would require (e.g. scoring predicted precision-support / partial-correlation recovery rather than mean overlay) and show, even as a small proof-of-concept, that it distinguishes two predictions with identical means but different covariance. Reviewers reward a diagnosed-blindspot-plus-fix over a diagnosis alone.

Resolve A3 per your own gate: either reframe it strictly as marginal-mean non-additivity (a data-level claim) with the mean-matched-null control, or drop it. As a standalone "causal regime where additive underperforms" claim it risks being Wall-1 restated and scored 4, dragging the paper's novelty down.

Name the scoop-risk labs in your own risk section and time accordingly: Arc Institute (STATE/VCC/PerturbHD-adjacent) and the Lotfollahi group (gene-expression generative eval) each hold most building blocks; a causal-recovery track added to the Virtual Cell Challenge would be a direct scoop. This argues for a fast preprint with the substitutability axis and the two-VCM non-overlay result locked in.

findings.weakness: No positive control passes: against the non-circular anchored ground truth, REAL interventional Perturb-seq itself scores at or below the permutation null, so the substitutability comparison has no working reference to substitute FOR. In results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json real_data F1=0.0314+/-0.004 sits at 28% of the 9.6% co-partition ceiling, and in results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json real_data is BELOW the permutation mean at every scale (N=500: 0.0346 vs null 0.0405; N=1000: 0.0068 vs null 0.0201; N=1500: 0.0125 vs null 0.0172; real_above_null=false everywhere). The a1 note itself concedes 'No condition separates from real or from ElasticNet.'
findings.why_it_matters: The headline claim 'VCM-predicted data cannot substitute for real Perturb-seq in causal discovery' presupposes that real Perturb-seq recovers structure. It does not, at any scale run. When both operands of the comparison are statistically indistinguishable from noise, the ordering real > VCM is not a measurement of VCM inadequacy but of a task that is unidentifiable at this SNR. The negative result is Type-II-limited (absence of a passing positive control), not an established absence — a reviewer cannot distinguish 'VCMs are useless' from 'the whole instrument recovers nothing.'
findings.evidence: /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json (real 0.0314, ceiling 0.0961, note line 188); /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json (real_above_null=false at N=500/1000/1500)

findings.weakness: Construct-validity mismatch between the ground truth and the estimand. The anchored GT is a perturbation-DE reachability set (edges = 'perturbing X significantly changes Y', q0.05/fc0.5), i.e. a total-effect / ancestral relation, while GIES and the inspre precision-support backends estimate a direct-edge Markov/CPDAG structure. These are different graph objects (transitive-closure vs sparse direct), so even a perfectly recovered causal graph need not match the anchored edge set. Corroborating evidence: the purely correlational GRNBoost2 (F1=0.0664+/-0.0027) OUTSCORES every causal backend including real-GIES against this GT.
findings.why_it_matters: If a co-expression method beats the causal-discovery backends on the 'ground truth', the GT rewards correlation, not causal structure — so F1 against it does not measure causal recovery at all. The floor-saturation may therefore be an artifact of scoring direct-edge estimators against a total-effect GT, which deflates every method equally and cannot separate 'method failed' from 'wrong graph type.' The benchmark's core instrument is not validated to measure the quantity the paper's thesis is about.
findings.evidence: /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json (grnboost2 0.0664 > cpa/gears/geneformer/real ~0.031-0.036; GT='perturbation_anchored... 1663 edges')

findings.weakness: The overlay is a confounded evaluation channel, not a neutral rescale: overlay conditions systematically OUTPERFORM the cell-level data they are derived from. At genome scale overlay_resampled_real beats real (N=500: 0.0559 vs 0.0346; N=1000: 0.0227 vs 0.0068), and in results/instrument_fix/c_cell_level_norman.json D_overlay_pred (0.0231) > A_real_cells (0.0164) > C_pred_cells (0.0113). A degraded/rescaled copy scoring above the original indicates the resampling reference pool is injecting its own covariance template into 'recovery.'
findings.why_it_matters: The Wall-1 lemma is used two ways at once: to argue the overlay is blind to a prediction's joint structure, AND as a valid counterfactual arm in A2/genome-scale/Track-C. But if overlay recovers MORE than the real cells, then overlay-based 'recovery' partly measures the resampler, not the biology or the predictor — invalidating every overlay condition as a stand-in for the real/predicted data and undermining the null contrasts built on it (e.g. the C-cell-level 'overlay is not the bottleneck' rebuttal).
findings.evidence: /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json (overlay > real at N=500/1000); /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/instrument_fix/c_cell_level_norman.json (D_overlay_pred 0.0231 > A_real 0.0164 > C_pred 0.0113)

findings.weakness: The Wall-1 invariance lemma is exact only in a deterministic no-sampling limit that never occurs in the actual evaluation. results/hardening/wall1_invariance.json shows Jaccard=1.0 / corr-delta 2.07e-8 only in part1 (deterministic); under the Poisson count sampling the pipeline actually uses, at realistic depth (depth_factor 1.0, ~2320 counts/cell) the test-retest support_jaccard collapses to 0.353 and edge_flip_fraction is 0.482 — nearly half the recovered edges flip between resamplings of the SAME input.
findings.why_it_matters: In the operating regime, the instrument's own test-retest reliability is ~0.35 Jaccard / ~48% edge churn. Every 'sharp' finding in the paper is a within-metric contrast of F1 differences on the order of 0.001-0.02, which is far inside this sampling-flip noise band. A measurement whose retest reliability is 0.35 cannot rank methods separated by <0.02, so the ordering claims (real vs VCM vs overlay, A vs B) are not supportable at the depth the benchmark runs, and the clean deterministic lemma does not transfer to that regime.
findings.evidence: /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/hardening/wall1_invariance.json (part2 depth=1.0: support_jaccard 0.353, edge_flip 0.482; verdict 'substantial at realistic depth')

findings.weakness: The benchmark's minimum detectable effect exceeds the magnitude of most effects it adjudicates. The A3 discriminator advertises MDE ~0.0047, yet the Track-B keep/discard decisions turn on effects smaller than that: OT B2 mean_gain +0.0019 (results/phaseB/b2_ot_definitive.json), J2 cov_reg-recon -0.0037 (results/phase4/j2_covariance_regularizer.json), B-proxy sweep -0.0026 (results/phaseB/b_precision_objective_sweep.json). Seed variance compounds this — partition_fair real_data sd 0.0277 on a mean of 0.0321 (CV ~86%) at 5 seeds.
findings.why_it_matters: When the design's own resolution floor (0.0047) is larger than the effects being called 'null/DISCARD' (0.001-0.004), those DISCARD verdicts are underpowered non-detections, not demonstrated absences of effect. The paper presents a chain of nulls (Track B objectives, instrument-fix cell-level, J2) as affirmative evidence that 'nothing helps,' but the instrument cannot resolve effects of the size in question, so the nulls carry little evidential weight for the negative thesis.

findings.weakness: The only at-scale, well-powered VCM measurement (20 seeds) is the circular-GT result, which is tautological by construction. results/multi_seed/aggregated_results.json gives real=1.0 because the GT is GIES-on-real (real is compared to structure derived from itself); VCMs score ~0.39-0.41 only because they are not literally the real data. When circularity is removed (anchored GT, a1), real collapses to 0.031 and the real-vs-VCM gap vanishes (circular/anchored Jaccard overlap = 0.017).
findings.why_it_matters: The paper's rhetorical weight is inverted relative to evidence quality: the 20-seed 'headline' is an artifact the paper itself now disowns, while the honest anchored regime that carries the thesis is 5-seed and floor-saturated. The real>VCM ordering survives ONLY in the circular regime where 'real cannot be substituted' is trivially true for anything that is not the exact input. This is not evidence of VCM inadequacy for discovery.
findings.evidence: /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/multi_seed/aggregated_results.json (real=1.0 by construction); /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json (circular_gt_overlap jaccard 0.0173, real collapses to 0.0314)

findings.weakness: The general conclusion is extrapolated beyond the scope actually run for VCMs, and the strongest rhetorical claim is the thinnest experiment. VCMs (CPA/GEARS/Geneformer/STATE) are scored non-circularly only in A1/A3; A2 (external TRRUST GRN) and the genome-scale axis have NO deep-VCM arm (only real/ElasticNet/overlay). The genome-scale 'last avenue closed' result is single-seed (seeds:[42]), uses a closed-form G=I-(ACE+eI)^-1 proxy rather than the pre-registered inspre ADMM (which the memo concedes 'could in principle separate where the closed form doesn't'), and has no SD.
findings.why_it_matters: The sweeping claim 'VCMs, real or predicted, do not recover structure above chance' rests on VCM data in only 2 of 6 armor axes; the genome-scale negative constrains only real data and transfers to VCMs by assumption. Meanwhile the multi-seed discipline that correctly overturned the B/B2 false positive (3-seed KEEP -> 5-seed DISCARD) was NOT applied to the n=1, proxy-backend experiment doing the most argumentative work — so the 'fully-armored negative' has its weakest joint exactly where it claims to be strongest.
findings.evidence: /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json (seeds:[42], backend 'G=I-ACE^-1', no VCM arm); /Users/aayanalwani/virtual cells/results/hardening/a2_external_grn.json (only real/overlay/elastic_net conditions)

findings.weakness: The A3 mechanism recast is partly confirmatory-by-construction and leans a positive mechanistic claim on an equivalence (absence-of-effect) result. In results/hardening/a3_mechanism_discriminator.json the surviving 5/5 contrast A-B (+0.0207) measures exactly the joint structure that condition B was DESIGNED to destroy, so A>B is guaranteed, not discovered. The only non-constructed contrast, B vs C, is -0.00136 with n_win 1/5 (TOST-equivalent) — an equivalence being reinterpreted as the positive claim 'the advantage lives in cell-level joint structure the overlay cannot access,' at a gap (0.0014) below the design's own MDE.
findings.why_it_matters: A constructed contrast plus an equivalence does not identify a mechanism. 'B destroys joint structure, therefore A-B measures joint structure' is circular; and using B~=C (a failure to detect a difference) to affirmatively locate the effect in the cell-level path overstates what an underpowered equivalence can establish. The one salvaged 'positive control' in the paper is thus a weaker inference than presented.

findings.weakness: The load-bearing conceptual frame — abstract per-gene metrics don't measure real VCM utility, so evaluate VCMs on a downstream scientific task — is not new. PerturbHD ('Are Current AI Virtual Cell Models Useful for Scientific Discovery?', bioRxiv 2026.04.23, Arc-adjacent) published exactly this thesis on the same VCM targets, differing only in which downstream (hit discovery vs. causal recovery). PerturbCausal reduces, at the framing level, to 'PerturbHD, but the downstream is GRN recovery.'
findings.why_it_matters: A benchmark paper's primary novelty IS its framing. If a reviewer knows PerturbHD, the 'utility not metrics' pitch reads as a downstream-choice variation of an already-published thesis, not a new idea. Worse, the manuscript's differentiating sentence ('None of these works has integrated VCM predictions into a downstream causal discovery pipeline') is technically true only because PerturbHD is scoped out — and PerturbHD is entirely absent from the 41-key bibliography, so the positioning is built against a citation set that omits its nearest conceptual competitor.
findings.evidence: Intro (line 100-138) cites ahlmanneltze2025, mejia2025diversity, miller2025calibrated, chevalley2025causalbench, vc_causal_2025 but the full cite-key dump contains NO PerturbHD / 'useful for scientific discovery' / utility-benchmark key. Scan flags PerturbHD as the single most dangerous concurrent work (same thesis, same targets, already flagged the rescaling artifact adjacent to the lemma).

findings.weakness: The overlay-invariance lemma — one of the two headline contributions — is an elementary linear-algebra fact (a positive-diagonal congruence of Sigma^{-1} preserves precision support) presented as a novel proved result, when the general theorem it instantiates is already published: 'When Marginals Match but Structure Fails: Covariance Fidelity in Generative Models' (arXiv 2603.17041) proves generically that matching all marginals leaves covariance unconstrained and flips downstream regression/PCA (Davis-Kahan). The lemma is a corollary, not a theorem.
findings.why_it_matters: The paper's theoretical mass rests on this lemma (it is cited ~8 times, motivates the covariance-regularizer null, and anchors the positive control). Proving it from scratch with no reference to the published general result inflates the theoretical contribution and hands a reviewer aware of 2603.17041 a clean 'this is a known corollary' rejection. The scan explicitly says: present it as a corollary of this line of work, not a novel theorem — the manuscript does the opposite.
findings.evidence: Line 453 gives the from-scratch proof ('positive diagonal congruence of Sigma^{-1} preserves the sign and zero/nonzero status'); Lemma stated at line 447-450. arXiv 2603.17041 is absent from the cite-key list. Manuscript's own contribution text (line 126) already concedes 'the calibration itself is a standard technique.'

findings.weakness: The empirical benchmark is a one-line extension of CausalBench (Chevalley et al., ICLR 2023): 'run the same causal-discovery backend on VCM-predicted counts instead of real counts.' CausalBench already supplies the task, the exact datasets (RPE1, K562), and the interventional metrics; the substitutability run is the obvious next experiment, and the manuscript itself concedes PerturbCausal 'is downstream of all of these.'
findings.why_it_matters: Low replication barrier equals high scoop exposure. Any of three groups (CausalBench authors, an inspre/IV team, or Arc) can execute the substitutability run on the existing platform in weeks. The single new axis over CausalBench is 'predicted-substitutes-for-real,' which is precisely the extension the CausalBench challenge infrastructure is built to accept. The novel moat therefore cannot be the task; it has to be the lemma + non-circular anchored GT — both of which are separately weak (finding 2) or thin (finding 6).
findings.evidence: Line 138 ('PerturbCausal is downstream of all of these') and Line 100 intro name CausalBench as prior real-data causal benchmark; scan rates CausalBench MEDIUM scoop as 'the platform a competitor would use to scoop you.'

findings.weakness: Arc Institute holds every building block AND the framing: STATE (a system under test), the perturbation atlas, the Virtual Cell Challenge benchmarking infrastructure, and the PerturbHD-adjacent utility-benchmark framing. A 'causal-recovery track' added to the VCC would be a direct, higher-powered scoop by the one actor that owns all inputs.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the dominant scoop threat and it is institutional, not incremental — no reframing removes it. Estimated probability roughly 35-50% over the next 6-12 months that Arc (or a VCC collaborator) publishes a causal/structure-recovery evaluation of VCMs, given they have already published the utility-benchmark framing and control the models, data, and challenge platform. If they do, they will do it at more seeds and more VCM arms than the current thin evidence base supports. Speed to publication and a sharp causal-specific angle are the only defenses.
