findings.weakness: The reframe moved the central claim INTO the most-owned adjacent territory rather than away from it. The pivot relocated the thesis onto (a) the covariance-fidelity result (arXiv 2603.17041) via the overlay-invariance lemma and (b) mode-collapse-to-the-mean (mejia2025diversity / PertEval-scFM), both actively owned by other groups. The paper even describes its own signature finding as 'the pooled-scope counterpart of the mode collapse reported elsewhere' — a re-observation, not a discovery.
findings.why_it_matters: Post-pivot the novelty distance to prior art shrank: the two spaces the reframed thesis now depends on are exactly the two the scan flags as MEDIUM-HIGH scoop (covariance-fidelity math already published; mode-collapse community 'owns collapse to the mean' and would overlap Wall-1 if they add a downstream causal task). The self-corrective pivot that fixed the circular-GT artifact simultaneously eroded conceptual originality.
findings.evidence: Abstract and line 383 explicitly frame the mode-collapse result as the 'pooled-scope counterpart of the mode collapse reported elsewhere (mejia2025diversity)'; overlay lemma (finding 2) is the 2603.17041 instantiation.

findings.weakness: The one genuinely non-recombined deliverable is a negative/substitutability verdict ('predicted data cannot substitute for real'), which is both the easiest kind of result to scoop with more compute and is under-powered exactly where the novelty needs to hold. Per the results state: the genome-scale 'last avenue closed' claim is single-seed (seed 42), two of six armor axes (A2 external-GRN, genome-scale) have no deep-VCM arm at all, and STATE — despite on-disk predictions — is never carried into the non-circular hardening axes.
findings.why_it_matters: A negative-result benchmark lives or dies on comprehensiveness and power. A competitor rerunning the identical pipeline with more seeds and a full VCM arm reaches the same 'no' with more authority and publishes the definitive version — the current paper's increment (the verdict) is the specific thing most vulnerable to being out-powered. The thin evidence means even the substitutability claim outruns its own VCM coverage, so the novel contribution is simultaneously scoopable and not yet fully earned.
findings.evidence: Results summary: genome_scale_precision.json is seeds:[42] only; a2_external_grn.json and genome-scale have 'VCM arm absent'; STATE predictions exist (data/model_outputs_real_n200_state/) but '0 mentions' in A1/A2 rescoring. Abstract's sweeping 'across every condition tested, no deep VCM exceeds a sparse linear comparator' rests on this uneven base.

findings.weakness: The most original component — the perturbation-anchored positive control that localizes recoverable signal in cell-level joint structure (real doubles' cells beat their own means-through-overlay 5/5, while non-additive vs additive means are TOST-equivalent) — is framed as a 'positive control,' not a headline contribution, and its distinctive content reduces to an equivalence (absence-of-effect) claim, part of which (condition B destroys joint structure by construction) is confirmatory-by-design.
findings.why_it_matters: The paper buries its single most novel experimental construction under 'control' framing while foregrounding the two weakest-for-novelty items (the CausalBench-extension benchmark and the corollary-grade lemma). The genuinely new idea is under-sold, and even it carries a novelty caveat: an equivalence claim with a positive interpretation is harder to defend as a contribution than a recovery-above-chance result would be. This is a positioning error that leaves the paper's originality less visible than it could be.
findings.evidence: Section sec:a3-localization (lines 470-472): A vs B +0.0207 5/5 is confirmatory-by-construction (B designed to destroy joint structure); only B vs C equivalence (TOST p=0.001) is a non-constructed contrast, and it is an equivalence claim. Positive control absolute F1 near permutation floor per results memo.

findings.weakness: The released benchmark provably cannot detect the improvement it exists to drive — it is self-neutralizing. The paper's own overlay-invariance Lemma (tex lines 447-455) proves the mean-overlay evaluation reduces any VCM prediction to its per-gene mean, so two predictions with identical means but 14.8x different gene-gene covariance yield the identical recovered graph (Jaccard 1.000). The paper then locates the entire residual substitutability gap in exactly that joint/covariance structure the metric cannot see, and shows its one proposed training-time fix (a covariance regularizer, released as a unit-tested module) produces a null 'because the metric never sees the predicted joint structure' (lines 455, 483). A VCM developer who genuinely improves joint structure scores identically.
findings.why_it_matters: A benchmark's entire value is discriminative power toward better models. This one is demonstrated, by its authors' own theorem, to be non-discriminative on the precise axis (covariance/precision support) the paper says governs the gap. Nobody can use it to show a model improved on the thing that matters, so it cannot serve as a leaderboard or a progress metric for VCM developers — the intended primary audience. The only fix (score predicted cells with covariance intact) is explicitly deferred to future work (line 482). The shipped artifact therefore has near-zero forward utility for driving model improvement.
findings.evidence: Lemma overlay-invariance (lines 447-455); covariance-regularizer null (line 383, app J2, +0.003+/-0.035, fails significance gate); conclusion concedes progress 'requires moving the evaluation channel' which is unexecuted (line 482). Open-sourced as a Python package (abstract) despite this.

findings.weakness: The ground truth is circular and fails external validation, so 'causal recovery' in this benchmark ranks fidelity to an algorithm artifact rather than to biology. Ground truth is per-backend GIES-on-real, self-defined (lines 152, 188). When scored against the literature-curated TRRUST network, GIES-on-real agrees at 0.83+/-0.70x a random-edge floor — a genuine null, not above chance (line 466). The technical-duplicate F1 between two GIES recoveries on disjoint real-cell halves is only 0.045 (line 466). The paper concedes the circularity 'remains a genuine limitation rather than one we have closed.'
findings.why_it_matters: The headline 'no VCM reaches real-data quality' reduces to 'no VCM reproduces GIES's own unvalidated artifact' — an artifact the paper itself shows does not encode verifiable regulatory biology. A causal-discovery researcher cannot change any practice on the basis of a ranking whose reference target is not shown to be real. This severs the link between the benchmark and the biological question that would give it significance; the result is actionable to no one downstream.
findings.evidence: TRRUST null (line 466, app:trrust); technical-duplicate 0.045 (Table main noise floor); ground truth defined as backend-on-real (lines 152, 188). Paper names a denser external gold standard as 'the priority extension' — i.e., not done.

findings.weakness: Every actionable path terminates in future work; the paper diagnoses but ships no validated remedy. The covariance regularizer is released but produces a null, and its 'definitive' end-to-end retrain 'requires GPU training' and is not run (line 383). The forward recommendation to move the evaluation channel is unexecuted (line 482). The positive control that 'localizes' the recoverable signal is partly confirmatory-by-construction — condition B is designed to destroy joint structure, so A-minus-B measuring a joint-structure effect is near-tautological (lines 470-472), leaving only the B~C equivalence as a genuine contrast.
findings.why_it_matters: Changing practice requires a next action that works. Here a VCM developer gets no validated training recipe (the one offered nulls out) and a causal-discovery researcher gets no usable substitution pipeline. The deliverable is a problem statement plus a lemma about why the tool cannot measure the solution — an insight, not an instrument. The impact ceiling is set by the fact that the reader's rational response is 'wait for the version where they actually change the eval channel and re-run,' which the paper has not produced.
findings.evidence: Covariance-regularizer null and un-run GPU retrain (line 383); eval-channel fix deferred (line 482); positive-control B constructed to null joint structure (line 470).

findings.weakness: The finding confirms what both target audiences' own literatures already imply, and the single new theoretical object is a corollary of already-published mathematics. VCMs' failure to beat linear baselines and their mode collapse to the mean are established results (Ahlmann-Eltze 2025 Nature Methods; Mejia 2025; the Virtual Cell Challenge). This paper ports the identical verdict one step downstream to causal recovery and gets the identical answer — no deep model beats ElasticNet (lines 222, 478). Per the novelty scan, the CI-invariance / overlay-invariance results are an application-only instance of the published covariance-fidelity theorem (arXiv 2603.17041, 'When Marginals Match but Structure Fails').
findings.why_it_matters: High-impact work redirects a research roadmap. VCM developers were already fighting mode collapse; causal-discovery researchers were already using real data because predicted data is known to be collapsed. Confirming an expected negative in a new task, with a lemma that is a specialization of published math, changes no one's plan. The ceiling as framed is 'useful confirmation and a cautionary note,' not a field pivot — and confirmations of the expected are the least-cited category of negative result.
findings.evidence: Same-verdict downstream port (lines 115, 137, 222, 478); lemma as corollary of arXiv 2603.17041 (novelty scan, 'HIGH on the lemma's core math').

findings.weakness: The paper debunks a practice it never shows anyone is performing. The motivating perspective paper (Callahan 2025) 'proposed a causal-evaluation taxonomy without implementation' and 'called explicitly for causal-recovery evaluation... but did not implement one' (lines 117, 143). No cited work actually feeds VCM-predicted Perturb-seq into a causal-discovery pipeline as a real substitute for real data. The motivation — substituting for 'CRISPR screens that cost millions' (lines 100, 115) — is aspirational, describing a use case that does not yet have practitioners.
findings.why_it_matters: A negative result's significance scales with how entrenched the mistaken practice is that it corrects. Foreclosing a hypothetical, not-yet-adopted workflow yields low urgency: no lab is currently being stopped from wasting money, because no lab is doing the substitution. A preemptive guardrail against a use case nobody has adopted is worth far less than a correction of active, resource-consuming error — and the paper never establishes the former exists.
findings.evidence: Motivating call-to-action never implemented by others (lines 117, 143); no citation of real VCM-into-causal-discovery substitution in practice; cost motivation is prospective (lines 100, 115).

findings.weakness: Low adoption ceiling: a single-lab, one-off diagnostic competing against incumbent, community-owned benchmarking infrastructure it cannot displace. The field's standard harnesses are PerturBench and the Virtual Cell Challenge (lines 115, 137), which hold far larger model rosters, the data, and the community; the paper's own roster (GEARS, CPA, Geneformer) predates the 2025-2026 model wave, and STATE was bolted on late and does not replicate (per-seed F1 = 0.462, 0.041, 0.048, lines 457-462). The natural home for a causal-recovery track is the VCC itself, and its eval channel is provably blind (Finding 1), so PerturbCausal cannot even function as a competing leaderboard.
findings.why_it_matters: Benchmarks have impact only through adoption and standardization. A blind-channel diagnostic with a stale roster, released by one group, has minimal chance of becoming the field's standard against incumbents (Arc Institute) that own the flagship models, the atlas, and the challenge infrastructure — the novelty scan rates this institutional scoop/obsolescence risk 'HIGH.' The likely outcome is a cited-once cautionary paper, not durable infrastructure the community builds on.
findings.evidence: Incumbent harnesses (lines 115, 137); stale roster + late STATE non-replication (lines 178, 457-462); novelty scan HIGH institutional scoop risk (Arc/VCC).

findings.weakness: The paper spends its heaviest empirical power on a result it now disowns, leaving the load-bearing reframed claims on thin evidence. The 20-seed circular-GT suite (results/multi_seed/aggregated_results.json) is by far the most-powered arm, yet the reframe argues that headline is an artifact of the blind channel. The current thesis rests on 5-seed (A1/A2/A3, positive control), 3-seed (STATE, initial OT), and single-seed evidence — the genome-scale 'last open avenue closed' claim runs on seed 42 only, using a closed-form screen rather than the pre-registered inspre ADMM (per results ledger).
findings.why_it_matters: A benchmark that aims to be definitive needs its central claim carried by its strongest evidence. Here the power distribution is inverted: the disowned result is 20-seed and the field-shifting claim is 1-to-5 seed. Reviewers and downstream readers will rationally treat the reframed conclusion as provisional and defer uptake until a higher-power, corrected-channel version appears — directly depressing near-term impact and citation.
findings.evidence: 20-seed disowned circular headline vs 5/3/1-seed reframed claims; genome-scale single-seed closed-form proxy, ADMM never run (results ledger, genome_scale_precision.json).

findings.weakness: The central claim "no VCM reaches real-data quality" has no non-degenerate regime: it is tautologically true under the circular GIES-on-real ground truth and empirically false/vacuous under the non-circular anchored one. Under Track A the ground truth IS the real-data GIES output, so real F1=1.0 by construction and 'no model reaches real-data quality' merely restates that no model perfectly reproduces GIES-on-real — a definitional identity, not a finding. Under the anchored GT that is supposed to rescue it, real collapses to F1≈0.031 and the VCMs sit at 0.031–0.036, i.e. VCMs statistically TIE real data (overlapping CIs) — so on the non-circular yardstick VCMs DO reach real-data quality, the opposite of the headline.
findings.why_it_matters: The paper's title and abstract claim ('VCM predictions do not substitute for real Perturb-seq') is the entire contribution. If the claim is a tautology on the circular GT and reversed on the non-circular GT, there is no ground on which it is both true and meaningful. A hostile reviewer will collapse the paper to: 'either you compared to your own algorithm's output, or everything (including real data) is at chance and the models are indistinguishable.'
findings.evidence: results/multi_seed/aggregated_results.json (real=1.0 by construction, Track A); results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json real_data f1_mean 0.0314±0.004 vs cpa 0.0359, gears 0.0358, geneformer 0.0352, elastic_net 0.0327 — all overlapping; note field: 'No condition separates from real or from ElasticNet.'

findings.weakness: The yardstick has no positive control: GIES-on-real is never shown to recover ANY independent biology above chance, so 'real-data quality' is quality-at-matching-an-unvalidated-algorithm-output. Against the curated TRRUST v2 network the recovered real-data graph agrees at 0.97±0.45x a random-edge floor (median p=0.36), and two GIES recoveries on disjoint real-cell halves agree at only F1=0.045. The paper itself concedes 'the circularity of the Track-A ground truth therefore remains a genuine limitation rather than one we have closed.' A benchmark whose reference network matches nothing external above chance cannot license any comparative verdict — positive or negative — because there is no established target to be close to or far from.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the load-bearing assumption of the whole benchmark. If the gold standard has zero demonstrated agreement with real regulatory biology, then 'VCMs fail to match real data' and 'the task is under-determined' are both statements about reproducing an unidentified estimator, not about causal biology. The honest disclosure in the limitations does not repair this — it concedes the benchmark's biological validity is unproven, which is exactly the attack.
findings.evidence: results/hardening/a2_external_grn.json trrust_lift_mean 0.9746±0.453, trrust_p_median 0.359, technical-duplicate F1=0.045; CausalCellBench_ICML_reframed.tex line 466 ('not robustly above chance ... remains a genuine limitation rather than one we have closed').

findings.weakness: The 'non-circular' anchored ground truth is a marginal differential-expression / total-effect graph, not a direct-causal DAG, so scoring a direct-edge discovery algorithm (GIES) against it is a category error that alone explains the universal F1≈0.03 floor. Edge A→B is defined as 'perturbing A significantly shifts B's marginal expression' (Welch t + BH-FDR + |log2FC|≥0.5) — by do-calculus this is the TOTAL effect P(B|do A)≠P(B), i.e. reachability/transitive closure of the true DAG, dominated for this ESSENTIAL-gene panel (ribosomal/proteasomal/mito) by generic pleiotropic stress co-regulation. GIES returns DIRECT parent→child edges; most anchored 'edges' are indirect and structurally unrecoverable as direct edges, capping recall before any data-quality question. The smoking gun: GRNBoost2 — a non-causal correlation/tree method — scores the HIGHEST F1 on the anchored 'causal' GT (0.066 vs real-GIES 0.031), i.e. the GT rewards association/total-effect over direct causal structure. This target-mismatch, not 'the task is under-determined,' is the real driver of the reframe.
findings.why_it_matters: The paper's flagship reframe ('per-perturbation causal recovery from Perturb-seq, real or predicted, is not identifiable at these scales') generalizes a metric artifact into a claim about causal identifiability. The near-zero F1 is the expected consequence of scoring direct-edge recovery against an indirect-effect gold standard on a maximally pleiotropic gene set — undisclosed, and it undercuts every downstream 'above chance' comparison.
findings.evidence: results/hardening/a1_anchored_gt_build.json (Welch t + BH-FDR + |log2FC| construction, essential-gene panel); prereg_A1_anchored_gt.md lines 11-16; results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json grnboost2 f1_mean 0.0664 > real_data 0.0314 (correlation method beats causal method on the 'causal' GT).

findings.weakness: GIES's identifiability preconditions are systematically violated by single-cell Perturb-seq, so 'real-data GIES output' is not an identifiable causal graph even in the infinite-sample limit — the paper never engages the standard causal-discovery assumptions. (1) Causal sufficiency: pervasive latent confounders (cell cycle, cell size, capture efficiency, batch) are unmodeled common causes. (2) Faithfulness: homeostatic feedback/buffering causes path cancellation, so the I-MEC is not faithfully identified. (3) Acyclicity: real GRNs are cyclic; the DAG search space cannot represent the target object. (4) Score misspecification: GIES uses a Gaussian BIC score applied to log1p over-dispersed zero-inflated counts. (5) Interventions: CRISPRi is a soft, variable-efficiency knockdown, not a perfect do() with known target — degrading the interventional edge orientation GIES relies on.
findings.why_it_matters: Under these joint violations the GIES-on-real graph is provably not identifiable as the true causal structure, so it cannot serve as ground truth regardless of circularity or the total-vs-direct issue. This is the deepest form of the yardstick problem: the estimator has no identifiability guarantee in the regime it is deployed, so its output is an artifact of misspecification as much as of biology.
findings.evidence: CausalCellBench_ICML_reframed.tex line 443 concedes the score-based faithful-limit caveat but only to argue calibration invariance; no treatment of confounding, cyclicity, count-likelihood misspecification, or soft-intervention identifiability anywhere in the methods.

findings.weakness: The recovery engine is run in a no-power regime, so 'real ≈ VCM ≈ ElasticNet ≈ chance' reflects estimator failure for ALL inputs rather than substitutability. Random 20-gene GIES partitioning imposes a 9.6%±1.2% co-partition ceiling — 90% of true edges are unrecoverable before any data question — and each fit sees ~100 cells for causal search over a 200-gene graph. When the instrument has no power, every data source collapses to chance, which is exactly the observation. The genome-scale screen shows REAL data itself at 0.85x/0.33x chance: if the instrument cannot detect signal in the gold data, it cannot adjudicate the VCM question at all. The paper's own minimum detectable effect (F1≈0.011–0.027) is comparable to the entire between-condition spread, so the 'no VCM reaches real-data quality' null is measured with an instrument admittedly near its own detection floor.
findings.why_it_matters: You cannot distinguish 'VCM predictions are as good as real data' from 'the discovery engine cannot tell any two inputs apart' — they produce identical data. The negative result is confounded with underpowered discovery, and there is no positive control demonstrating the engine CAN separate a planted structure from chance on this pipeline.
findings.evidence: results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json copartition_ceiling mean 0.0961±0.0118; results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json real_above_null=false at every N (0.85x, 0.33x, 0.95x chance); CausalCellBench_ICML_reframed.tex line 466 MDE 0.011–0.027.

findings.weakness: The strongest rhetorical claim ('the last open avenue closed,' 'the task is under-determined') rests on the weakest evidence: a single-seed (seed 42 only), closed-form G=I−(ACE+εI)⁻¹ proxy that is NOT the pre-registered inspre ADMM backend (scripts/genome_scale_inspre.py exists but was never run), with no VCM arm at all. The memo itself concedes 'the ADMM could in principle separate where the closed form doesn't.' The exact multi-seed significance discipline that correctly caught the Track-B/B2 false positive was not applied to the experiment doing the most argumentative work. Two of the six 'armor' axes (A2 and genome-scale) contain no deep-VCM arm, so 'no VCM reaches real-data quality' is extrapolated to VCMs never scored non-circularly at scale. A memo/artifact inconsistency compounds this: the memo states N=1500 did not complete, but the JSON holds a completed N=1500 real result (F1=0.0125, 0.95x chance).
findings.why_it_matters: A n=1, no-SD, no-VCM, admitted-proxy result cannot carry a claim framed as a general identifiability impossibility. The under-determination reframe is the paper's headline pivot from 'VCMs underperform' to 'the task itself has no signal' — and it is the least rigorously supported experiment in the suite.
findings.evidence: results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json seeds:[42], backend 'G=I-ACE^-1', conditions real/elastic_net/overlay only (no VCM); RESULTS_GENOME_SCALE.md ('ADMM could in principle separate ... deferred to proper compute'); scripts/genome_scale_inspre.py present with no result JSON.

findings.weakness: The A3 'positive control' that localizes the missing signal to cell-level joint structure is partly confirmatory-by-construction and infers a positive mechanism from an equivalence (absence) result. The secondary contrast A−B (+0.0207, 5/5) is circular: condition B is DEFINED as A with its joint structure removed, so 'A beats B ⟹ signal lives in joint structure' merely restates the construction. The only non-constructed contrast is B≈C (TOST p=0.001) — an absence-of-mean-channel-effect — from which a positive claim about a specific cell-level-joint-structure mechanism is drawn; absence of a mean effect is equally consistent with the anchored GT simply being insensitive to non-additive means. And the whole control is scored against the same DE/total-effect anchored GT (Finding 3), so 'recoverable interventional structure' again conflates direct and indirect effects.
findings.why_it_matters: This control is the one constructive, non-null result the paper leans on to say the missing signal is real and localizable. If its central inference is either circular (A−B) or an absence-as-presence over-read (B≈C), the paper loses its only positive anchor and is left entirely with nulls measured on an unvalidated yardstick.
findings.evidence: results/hardening/a3_mechanism_discriminator.json gate: BC_gain -0.00136 (equivalence) carrying verdict 'real advantage came from cell-path joint structure'; secondary_AB_gain 0.0207 where condition B design = 'real double MEANS through overlay (mean-matched null)' i.e. joint structure removed by construction; within-F1 for real_doubles = 1.0 by construction.

findings.weakness: The genome-scale 'last avenue closed' / 'fully-armored negative' claim rests on a SINGLE seed (seeds:[42]) with no standard deviation, no significance gate, no VCM arm, and a proxy backend (closed-form G=I−ACE⁻¹, not the pre-registered inspre ADMM). At the scales that matter, real interventional data itself recovers structure AT OR BELOW permutation chance: N=1000 real F1=0.0068 = 0.46x chance, N=1500 real=0.0125 = 0.95x chance, N=500 real=0.0346 with real_above_null=False (below perm p975=0.0741). You cannot conclude 'VCMs can't substitute at genome scale' from a regime where the estimator recovers nothing for anyone, tested at n=1, with no VCM ever run.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the paper's strongest rhetorical claim (the scale axis that answers foundation-model genome-scale ambitions) yet it is the least powered experiment in the suite. The exact multi-seed discipline that caught the false B/B2 KEEP was not applied here. A single seed cannot support 'the last open avenue is closed'; and because real data is sub-chance, the negative is confounded — it reflects a degenerate recovery regime, not a VCM-specific failure. A hostile reviewer kills the scale contribution on n=1 alone.
findings.evidence: results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json: seeds:[42]; N=1000 f1_over_random=0.46, N=1500=0.95, all real_above_null=false; conditions = real/elastic/overlay only (no CPA/GEARS/Geneformer/STATE). Memo concedes ADMM 'could in principle separate where the closed form doesn't.'

findings.weakness: The '20-seed heavily powered circular headline' is only n=20 for the baselines (real_data, elastic_net, overlay_resampled_real, grnboost2). The three actual VCMs under test — CPA, GEARS, Geneformer — were run on only the first 5 seeds [42,123,456,789,1024]; seeds 2048…2147483647 have just 4 conditions. Every substantive VCM claim in the paper, circular and non-circular, therefore rests on n=5. Worse, the reported comparison pools an n=5 VCM mean against an n=20 baseline mean over non-identical seed sets, and the resulting CIs overlap completely: CPA AUPRC 0.400 CI[0.373,0.428], GEARS 0.419, Geneformer 0.422, ElasticNet 0.411 CI[0.399,0.424], overlay 0.420. The whole 0.40–0.43 cluster is statistically indistinguishable.
findings.why_it_matters: The project map advertises the headline as '20 seeds, heavily powered,' but the power applies only to arms that are not the object of study. Substitutability is a VCM claim and it is n=5 everywhere. Because CPA/GEARS/Geneformer/ElasticNet/overlay CIs all overlap, the manuscript cannot support any ordering among them (e.g. 'VCM 0.40 < ElasticNet 0.42'); and comparing n=5 to n=20 across different seed draws is an unbalanced, non-paired contrast. This mislabels the evidentiary weight of the central result.
findings.evidence: results/multi_seed/aggregated_results.json: cpa/gears/geneformer n=5; elastic/overlay/grnboost/real n=20; per-seed condition lists show deep VCMs only on first 5 seeds. Overlapping CIs computed from the aggregated block.

findings.weakness: Nearly every non-circular effect sits AT or BELOW its own permutation chance floor, so the sign of the load-bearing contrasts is drawn from noise. A3 primary within-regime GT: real_doubles F1=0.0552 vs permutation chance 0.0795 (below chance); additive_singles 0.0359 vs 0.0534 (below chance) — 'real doubles beat additive-of-singles 5/5 seeds' compares two sub-chance quantities. Genome-scale real is sub-chance at N≥1000. A1 anchored partition-fair real F1=0.0321 with SD=0.0277 (per-seed 0.0075→0.0649, a ~9x spread), i.e. the standard deviation nearly equals the mean and the 5-seed interval effectively spans zero.
findings.why_it_matters: Recovery metrics that fall below the label-permutation floor are not measuring recovered structure; differences between two below-floor conditions are within-noise and not interpretable as 'VCM can/can't recover.' The memos disclose this, but the executive framings ('real beats additive 5/5', 'VCMs help / don't help') read as if the quantities are above chance. With SD≈mean at n=5, no separation between conditions is detectable in principle.
findings.evidence: results/hardening/a3_floor.json (real_doubles 0.0552<0.0795, additive 0.0359<0.0534); results/hardening/a1_anchored_rescore.json partition_fair real mean 0.0321 sd 0.0277 per_seed [0.0075,0.0649,0.0581,0.0076,0.0221]; genome_scale real_above_null=false.

findings.weakness: The keep/discard gates are underpowered seed-votes, not significance tests, and this drives the reframe's null/DISCARD verdicts. The 'definitive GPU' Sinkhorn KEEP was a 3/3 seed vote with mean_gain 0.0038, sd 0.0032 (n=3 → t≈2.0, p≈0.18, non-significant) that then failed at 5 seeds (B2: +0.0019, 3/5). A 3/5 vote has p≈0.5 under a fair coin; a 3/3 vote p≈0.125. The instrument-fix nulls (RPE1 T2 1/5, Norman T2 3/5) and Track-B nulls are all n=5 with per-difference SDs ~0.005–0.01, giving a minimum detectable effect ≈0.005 — larger than the effect magnitudes being ruled out. 'NULL/DISCARD' at n=5 cannot separate a true zero from a small real effect.
findings.why_it_matters: The entire reframe ('the overlay/instrument is not the bottleneck', 'objectives don't help') is a stack of underpowered null claims. The B→B2 flip is direct proof the gate accepts noise. Declaring the central negative 'armored' from a battery of n=5 nulls confuses absence of evidence with evidence of absence, and the seed-vote gate has no calibrated Type-I/Type-II control.
findings.evidence: results/phaseB/b_gpu_definitive.json gates.sinkhorn keep=true n_win=3/3 mean_gain 0.0038 sd 0.0032; results/phaseB/b2_ot_definitive.json confirmatory_gate keep=false n_win=3/5; results/instrument_fix/c_cell_level_*.json T-gates 1/5–3/5; a3_floor MDE-scale ~0.005.

findings.weakness: The mean-preserving overlay resample — the supposedly-blind 'neutral' control that anchors the SNR interpretation — consistently OUTSCORES real data on every non-circular axis. Genome scale: overlay beats real at all sizes (N=500 0.0559 vs 0.0346; N=1000 0.0227 vs 0.0068; N=1500 0.0184 vs 0.0125), with higher lift-over-random. A2 external GRN: overlay TRRUST lift 1.28 vs real 0.97 (real recovers a curated GRN BELOW chance). Circular headline: overlay AUPRC 0.420 is the highest non-real condition, above ElasticNet and every VCM. If a mean-only, structure-free resample beats real interventional data, the F1 recovery estimand is tracking edge-count/degree artifacts, not causal structure.
findings.why_it_matters: The overlay-invariance lemma frames the overlay as carrying no extra structure, so it should be a floor, not a ceiling. Its systematically higher score means the recovery metric is not a valid readout of causal-structure fidelity at these scales — which invalidates using 'real vs predicted F1' as the yardstick for substitutability and undermines the interpretation of the genome-scale negative. The estimand does not track ground truth.
findings.evidence: results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json overlay>real at every N; results/hardening/a2_external_grn.json trrust_lift_mean overlay 1.275 > real 0.975; results/multi_seed/aggregated_results.json overlay AUPRC 0.4204 top non-real.

findings.weakness: The A3 mechanism-discriminator TOST equivalence — used to positively assert 'the advantage lives in cell-level joint structure, not means' — passes mainly because its equivalence margin is set wide relative to n=5 precision. Margin = 0.01035, observed |B−C| = 0.00136, MDE_at_n5 = 0.0047; the margin is ~7.6x the observed difference and ~2.2x the minimum detectable effect, and appears scaled to the AB gap (0.0207) rather than to a pre-specified biologically-meaningful threshold. TOST_p=0.001 'EQUIVALENT' at n=5 with a margin twice the MDE is weak equivalence evidence, and it is being used to support a positive interpretation from an absence-of-difference.
findings.why_it_matters: Equivalence testing only supports 'no effect larger than the margin.' With n=5 and a margin chosen at the effect scale rather than pre-registered, the test is nearly guaranteed to pass and cannot exclude a null-relevant effect between the MDE (0.0047) and the margin (0.0104). Building the recast headline ('signal localizes to joint structure') on this inverts the logic of an equivalence result.
findings.evidence: results/hardening/a3_robustness.json TOST_BvsC mean_diff -0.00136, equivalence_margin 0.01035, MDE_at_n5 0.004703, AB_gap 0.0207; a3_mechanism_discriminator.json BC_gain -0.00136 n_win 1/5.

findings.weakness: No multiplicity control across a decision family of ~6 hardening axes × 3–7 conditions × multiple keep/discard gates, and the SAME 5 seeds [42,123,456,789,1024] are reused for A1, A2, A3, C (both datasets), B, B2, and J2. Cross-axis 'consistency' is therefore not independent evidence — the shared seeds fix the sampled cells/partitions across axes, correlating the errors. With ~10+ gates and no Bonferroni/FDR, at least one false KEEP/DISCARD is expected under the null; the B 3/3→B2 flip is a realized instance.
findings.why_it_matters: The paper's persuasive force comes from many axes 'all nulling the same way,' but reused seeds make those axes statistically dependent, and running a large family of underpowered gates without correction inflates the family-wise error of the keep/discard decisions. The reframe's 'everything points the same way' is partly an artifact of correlated draws plus uncorrected multiplicity.
findings.evidence: Identical seed list [42,123,456,789,1024] in a1_anchored_rescore, a2_external_grn, a3_mechanism_discriminator, c_cell_level_rpe1/norman, b2_ot_definitive; decision-ledger contains many gates with no FWER/FDR field.

findings.weakness: Scope/scale mismatch between the tested panel and the genome-scale foundation-model claim. VCMs (CPA/GEARS/Geneformer/STATE) are evaluated only on an N=200 K562-essential panel; the scaling axis (N≤1500) has no VCM arm and n=1. Extrapolating 'VCMs, real or predicted, don't recover structure above chance at scale' from N=200 VCM evidence + a single-seed no-VCM N=1500 run is a two-order-of-magnitude gap to true genome scale (~20k genes). Separately, on the non-circular anchored GT the correlation-based GRNBoost2 scores F1=0.0664 — roughly double real interventional data (0.0314) and every deep VCM — which flags the anchored ground truth as biased toward co-expression rather than causal structure (a construct-validity problem for the yardstick itself).
findings.why_it_matters: Foundation-model genome-scale ambitions are answered with N=200 VCM data and an n=1 no-VCM scaling curve, so the strongest scale rhetoric outruns the tested regime. And if a purely correlational method beats real interventional data on the 'non-circular' GT, the anchored GT is not cleanly measuring interventional/causal recovery, weakening every relative contrast built on it.
findings.evidence: b_gpu_definitive.json / a1 panel = 200 genes; genome_scale_precision.json max N=1500 seed 42, no VCM; a1_anchored_rescore.json grnboost2 f1_mean 0.0664 vs real 0.0314 vs cpa 0.0359.

ranked_pivots.name: Covariance-sensitive cell-output evaluation channel (escape the Wall-1 lemma)
ranked_pivots.description: Replace the mean-overlay channel (a positive-diagonal congruence that provably cannot reward predicted covariance) with a channel that consumes predicted single cells and scores a joint-structure-sensitive statistic: precision-matrix support recovery, conditional-independence / partial-correlation tests, or higher-moment discrepancy. This is the intellectual core: it makes the benchmark able, in principle, to register whether a VCM preserves the joint structure causal discovery needs, and directly separates PerturbCausal from PerturbHD (hit-ranking downstream) and from the mean-only debate. If predicted cells are mode-collapsed and still register blind, that becomes an affirmative, mechanism-level finding rather than an invariance artifact.
addresses: validity: overlay is a confounded channel (overlay > real cells)
validity: Wall-1 lemma holds only in deterministic no-sampling limit
validity: A3 mechanism recast is confirmatory-by-construction
novelty: reduces to PerturbHD-with-different-downstream at framing level
ranked_pivots.dependencies: covariance_preservation.py + training/covariance_regularizer.py modules already present; STATE/CPA/GEARS cell-level outputs on disk (model_outputs_real_n200_state); GPU box
ranked_pivots.risk: The new channel inherits the ~0.35 test-retest Jaccard at realistic depth, so it must be paired with the power/MDE redesign; and if the channel shows no VCM signal, the contribution shifts from 'measures utility' to 'diagnoses collapse'.

ranked_pivots.name: Reframe as an identifiability phase diagram (turn floor-saturation into the contribution)
ranked_pivots.description: Instead of an embarrassing null, make the map itself the deliverable: characterize WHEN predicted Perturb-seq can substitute for real by sweeping SNR / depth / perturbation strength / n-cells / gene-set size and plotting the identifiable region where real clears null and where VCM tracks real. This directly fills the gap arXiv 2605.04930 concedes as future work (linking recovery failure to real-data properties), reframes the negative from 'VCMs are useless' to 'here is the operating envelope for causal use of VCM data', and absorbs the retest-reliability and MDE results as axes rather than liabilities.
addresses: validity: Type-II-limited negative / no positive control
validity: MDE and retest reliability
novelty: PerturbHD framing overlap
novelty: differentiate from synthetic-only GRN-break diagnostic
ranked_pivots.dependencies: SNR/depth sweep harness (extend wall1 + genome_scale scripts), all backends, GPU box
ranked_pivots.risk: A phase diagram that shows the identifiable region is empty at achievable SNR is a weaker (though still honest) contribution; and 2605.04930 authors could extend to real data first — speed matters.

ranked_pivots.name: Positive method: covariance-regularized objective evaluated in the escaped channel
ranked_pivots.description: Convert 'nothing helps' into a method contribution by pairing the covariance-regularizer training module with the covariance-sensitive channel. The J2 cov_reg null (-0.0037) is expected under mean-only eval — the Wall-1 lemma proves a covariance penalty CANNOT register there. Re-test cov_reg under the joint-structure channel where it can register; if downstream causal utility improves above MDE, that is a genuine positive result ('here is what makes VCM output causally usable'). If still null with adequate power, that is a strong, non-tautological negative.
addresses: validity: MDE-limited DISCARD verdicts (J2 cov_reg)
validity: overlay channel cannot reward covariance
novelty: escape PerturbHD by shipping a method, not just a benchmark
ranked_pivots.dependencies: training/covariance_regularizer.py, calibration/covariance_preservation.py, the new cell-output channel (depends on the escape-the-lemma pivot), GPU box
ranked_pivots.risk: Highest-uncertainty pivot: the effect may remain null even under the fair channel if VCMs mode-collapse too hard to learn usable covariance; and it is gated on the escaped-channel pivot landing first.

ranked_pivots.name: Working positive control (SNR-restricted recoverable regime)
ranked_pivots.description: Before any substitutability claim, establish a sub-task where REAL Perturb-seq provably beats the permutation null: restrict to strong perturbations and high-coverage TF-target pairs, raise per-cell depth / pool cells, and pick the estimator-GT pairing where real clears null with a real_above_null=true margin > MDE. Only inside that identifiable window does the ordering real>VCM carry meaning. This is the single most load-bearing fix — the entire negative thesis is uninterpretable while both operands sit at noise (F1 0.031 vs 9.6% ceiling; real below null at every genome scale).
addresses: validity: no positive control passes (Type-II-limited negative)
validity: only well-powered result is circular-GT
validity: general conclusion extrapolated beyond scope
ranked_pivots.dependencies: A1/A3 anchored-GT code (scripts/a1_perturbation_anchored_gt.py), existing GIES/inspre backends, GPU box for re-scoring sweeps
ranked_pivots.risk: Real Perturb-seq may fail to clear null even in the restricted regime; if so the honest read is 'task unidentifiable at this SNR', which forces reframing (see phase-diagram pivot) rather than a VCM-inadequacy claim.

ranked_pivots.name: Match the graph object (estimand-GT alignment + correlational baseline)
ranked_pivots.description: The anchored GT is a total-effect / ancestral reachability set, but GIES and inspre estimate direct-edge CPDAG/precision support — different graph objects, which is why correlational GRNBoost2 (0.0664) beats every causal backend. Fix by making estimand and GT the same object: either (a) evaluate total-effect estimators (ACE / do-effect ranking) against the reachability GT, or (b) build a direct-edge GT (single-hop CRISPRi effects, mediation-pruned) for the direct-edge estimators. Always report the correlational baseline explicitly; if it still wins, the GT rewards correlation and must be rebuilt.
addresses: validity: construct-validity mismatch (total-effect GT vs direct-edge estimand)
validity: correlational GRNBoost2 outscores causal backends
ranked_pivots.dependencies: A3 anchored-GT builder, mediation/transitive-closure post-processing, GRNBoost2 baseline already run
ranked_pivots.risk: A properly matched GT may show causal backends still tie the correlational baseline on real data, which would concede that interventional structure is not recoverable here (echoes CausalBench Challenge) — still publishable but blunts the VCM angle.

ranked_pivots.name: Genome-scale done right: pre-registered inspre ADMM, multi-seed, with a VCM arm
ranked_pivots.description: The genome-scale 'last avenue closed' result is the strongest rhetorical claim but the weakest experiment: single-seed (seeds:[42]), a closed-form G=I-(ACE+eI)^-1 proxy instead of the pre-registered inspre ADMM, no SD, and no deep-VCM arm (only real/ElasticNet/overlay). Re-run at N=500/1000/1500 with the actual inspre ADMM backend (already scales to ~1000 genes), 5-20 seeds with SD, and add CPA/GEARS/Geneformer/STATE predicted inputs so the genome-scale conclusion actually constrains VCMs rather than transferring by assumption.
addresses: validity: genome-scale is n=1, proxy backend, no VCM arm
validity: general conclusion extrapolated beyond scope
validity: STATE omitted from non-circular axes
ranked_pivots.dependencies: scripts/genome_scale_inspre.py + phase5_inspre.py (inspre scaling asset), STATE outputs on disk, GPU box
ranked_pivots.risk: inspre ADMM may separate where the closed form did not (the memo concedes this), which could partially overturn the genome-scale negative — a real possibility that must be pre-registered, not steered around.

ranked_pivots.name: External / dual ground truth (ABC + CRISPRi enhancer-gene, TF-target)
ranked_pivots.description: Break circularity and construct-mismatch simultaneously by scoring against independently derived, mechanism-level maps: ABC-model and CRISPRi enhancer-gene links plus curated TF-target sets (TRRUST/ChIP), matched to the K562/RPE1 cell context. Report recovery against two orthogonal external GTs so no single GT's bias (correlation-rewarding, transitive-closure) drives the verdict. This also answers the reviewer objection that the anchored GT is home-built.
addresses: validity: construct-validity mismatch
validity: circular-GT is the only well-powered result
validity: STATE / coverage gaps
novelty: differentiate from circular self-comparison
ranked_pivots.dependencies: a2_external_grn harness (extend beyond TRRUST), public ABC/CRISPRi K562 enhancer-gene datasets, cell-line matching
ranked_pivots.risk: Enhancer-gene maps are sparse and cell-line-specific; low overlap with the perturbed gene panel could leave the external GT underpowered too, reproducing the floor-saturation rather than escaping it.

ranked_pivots.name: Fix the retest-reliability / MDE floor (power-first redesign)
ranked_pivots.description: The instrument's realistic-depth test-retest support Jaccard is 0.353 with 48% edge churn, and the advertised MDE (~0.0047) exceeds most effects being adjudicated as DISCARD (B2 +0.0019, J2 -0.0037, B-sweep -0.0026). Raise reliability by increasing sequencing depth / cell pooling and averaging support over resamplings (consensus edges), compute the true MDE per axis, scale seeds until power is adequate for the target effect size, and report DISCARD verdicts honestly as TOST equivalence only when the effect is inside a pre-registered negligible band. Reclassify underpowered nulls as 'inconclusive'.
addresses: validity: Wall-1 test-retest 0.35 Jaccard swamps <0.02 contrasts
validity: MDE exceeds effects adjudicated as null
validity: A3 B~C equivalence over-read as positive
ranked_pivots.dependencies: wall1_invariance harness, existing multi-seed infrastructure, GPU box
ranked_pivots.risk: Honest reclassification will likely turn several current DISCARD 'nothing helps' claims into 'inconclusive', shrinking the affirmative negative-result footprint the paper currently leans on.

ranked_pivots.name: Reposition as an evaluation-standard contribution + honest scope/citation repair
ranked_pivots.description: Package the invariance lemma (as an explicit corollary of arXiv 2603.17041, not a novel theorem) plus the covariance-sensitive channel into a reusable 'causal-substitutability score' standard for VCMs, and repair positioning: cite PerturbHD prominently and differentiate on downstream (causal recovery vs hit-ranking) + the lemma + genome-scale under-determination, cite the mode-collapse and Ahlmann-Eltze lines as the backdrop, and fix stale docs (RESULTS.md STUB claims, missing STATE in non-circular axes, under-reported N=1500). This is the cheapest defense against the HIGH framing-scoop risk.
addresses: novelty: PerturbHD framing scoop (absent from bibliography)
novelty: lemma is application-only vs 2603.17041
validity: stale/contradictory docs and STATE omission
ranked_pivots.dependencies: existing manuscript, bibliography, RESULTS memos; no compute needed
ranked_pivots.risk: Positioning alone cannot rescue the empirical validity problems; honest scoping will also narrow several current claims, so it must ship alongside the substantive fixes, not instead of them.

ranked_pivots.name: Multi-seed cross-dataset replication (RPE1 + K562 + Norman + Replogle)
ranked_pivots.description: Cheap credibility hardening: run the corrected (non-circular, matched-GT) pipeline across at least three real Perturb-seq datasets with 10-20 seeds and report SD/CIs everywhere. phase7_crossdataset already has inspre on Norman and RPE1, so this extends an existing scaffold rather than building new. Demonstrates the verdict is not a single-dataset, single-seed artifact — the exact discipline that correctly flipped the B/B2 false KEEP to DISCARD.
addresses: validity: only well-powered result is circular
validity: coverage inconsistency across systems
validity: general conclusion beyond scope
ranked_pivots.dependencies: results/phase7_crossdataset (norman/rpe1 inspre already run), Replogle/K562 loaders, GPU box
ranked_pivots.risk: Low; main risk is that cross-dataset variance is so large that no consistent ordering survives, which is itself an honest and reportable finding.

verdicts.pivot: Covariance-sensitive cell-output evaluation channel (escape the Wall-1 lemma)
verdicts.feasibility_reality: Assets are real and confirmed on disk: causalcellbench/calibration/covariance_preservation.py, causalcellbench/training/covariance_regularizer.py, tests/test_covariance_regularizer.py, and data/model_outputs_real_n200_state (plus norman/rpe1 cell-level outputs). GPU box available. The CHANNEL ENGINEERING (precision-support / partial-correlation / higher-moment statistic over predicted single cells) is genuinely a 2-3 week solo build — that estimate is honest for the code. But that is the easy 30%. The scientifically-valid version requires three prerequisites the pivot does not scope: (1) a positive control (a reference that clears the null on the new channel), (2) a ground truth whose estimand matches a precision-support / direct-edge object, and (3) the power/MDE redesign the risk note gestures at. Those are the actual project. 'medium' feasibility is right for the artifact, optimistic for the interpretable result.
verdicts.scoop_risk: Moderate. Distributional / joint-structure evaluation of perturbation-prediction models is already commodity machinery: scPerturb (Peidli/Theis) ships E-distance, PerturBench and CPA/scGen eval papers use MMD/energy-distance, and precision-matrix recovery is standard GGM. A reviewer can say 'you applied E-distance/GLASSO that scPerturb already does.' No named lab is obviously about to publish the exact framing (does covariance preservation change causal-discovery SUBSTITUTABILITY), which is narrower and defensible — but the novelty lives entirely in that framing, not in the statistic, so the framing must carry the paper. Theis/Helmholtz is the group with all the building blocks already public; flag ~25-35% that a distributional-eval-for-perturbation paper lands adjacent within a year, diluting the 'first to measure joint structure' claim.
hidden_failure_modes: The channel escapes the lemma but lands in the WORST-SNR regime, and the repo already proves it. Wall1 part2 IS the covariance channel: precision-matrix support_jaccard on the SAME input at realistic depth (2320 counts/cell) is 0.353 with 48% edge flip; even at 8x depth (18.5k counts) it is only 0.51 / 35% flip. Covariance/precision needs O(p) more samples than the mean, so scoring joint structure from mode-collapsed shallow single cells maximizes variance. Test-retest 0.35 cannot rank methods separated by 0.005-0.02 (A_real 0.0164 vs C_pred 0.0113). The pivot's own risk line concedes this. Escaping the lemma is necessary but the noise floor is fatal-on-arrival unless paired with the MDE/depth redesign FIRST.

The two CRITICAL failures are UPSTREAM of the channel and channel-switch does not touch them. No positive control: real Perturb-seq scores BELOW the permutation null at every genome scale (0.0346 vs 0.0405 null at N=500; below at 1000/1500). A covariance channel has no mechanism to manufacture a passing reference — if real data can't clear the null on the mean channel there is no prior that it clears a higher-variance covariance channel. Without a passing positive control the 'substitutability' thesis has no operand to substitute for, on ANY channel.

Construct-validity mismatch gets WORSE, not better. The anchored GT is a perturbation-DE reachability / total-effect (ancestral) set; precision-support and partial-correlation are the most explicitly DIRECT-edge Markov objects available. Scoring a direct-edge estimand against a transitive-closure GT is a larger graph-type mismatch than GIES already had — and correlational GRNBoost2 already outscores every causal backend against this GT, so the GT rewards correlation. The covariance channel inherits this and cannot separate 'method failed' from 'wrong graph object' until a matched GT is built.

Dropping overlay removes the arm the paper's current null-contrasts stand on. Overlay OUTSCORES real cells (D_overlay_pred 0.0231 > A_real 0.0164; genome-scale overlay 0.0559 > real 0.0346) — correctly flagged as a resampler-template confound — but the Track-C 'overlay is not the bottleneck' rebuttal and A2/genome-scale null contrasts are all overlay-built. The pivot silently forces a rebuild of those, expanding scope beyond 2-3 weeks.

Best-case honest outcome is a REFRAME, not a utility measurement. The pivot's own risk note admits: if the channel shows no VCM signal the contribution shifts from 'measures utility' to 'diagnoses collapse.' Given the below-null real data, that reframe is the likely outcome. It is a real, publishable negative/diagnostic result — but it is impact_delta ~+2 (a mechanism-level collapse diagnostic), not the +5 claimed (a working substitutability meter).
verdicts.verdict: Not busywork and not dead-on-arrival: the pivot correctly diagnoses that the mean-overlay channel is provably blind to exactly what the thesis is about, and part3 of wall1_invariance (pred off-diag corr 0.261 vs 0.018 from identical means) confirms the pipeline IS sensitive to joint structure when a channel reads it directly. So it formally escapes the Wall-1 lemma — that channel-switch is the genuine intellectual core and the only move that separates PerturbCausal from PerturbHD. BUT escaping the lemma is necessary, not sufficient, and the pivot as scoped overstates impact (5, should be ~2) and understates the real work. It does NOT resolve the two CRITICAL upstream failures (no positive control — real data is below null; GT construct-mismatch — which the direct-edge precision estimand makes worse), and it lands the metric in the lowest-SNR regime the repo has already measured at 0.35 test-retest / 48% edge-flip. Re-scope before building: run ONE cheap gate first — does REAL Perturb-seq clear the permutation null on the covariance channel? If no, the substitutability paper is unidentifiable at this SNR and should be reframed now as a collapse-diagnostic (the honest, defensible contribution); do not build the full channel and rewrite the paper around 'measures utility' until that gate passes. Priority 4 for the gate experiment, contingent-downgrade to a reframe if it fails.

verdicts.pivot: Reframe as an identifiability phase diagram (turn floor-saturation into the contribution)
verdicts.verdict: Honest repackaging, not a rescue. The reframe is a better narrative posture but it does not fix any of the three fundamental instrument-validity failures, and its own central deliverable — the "identifiable region where real clears null" — is, on the repo's existing evidence, most likely EMPTY at every achievable regime. genome_scale_precision.json already shows real_above_null=false at N=500/1000/1500. A phase diagram needs at least one cell where real beats null to have a boundary to draw; the data run so far has none. So the sweep risks producing a map that is uniformly "not identifiable," which is the same null with prettier axes. Do the cheap writing-level reframe, but do NOT spend GPU-weeks substantiating a phase boundary that the current evidence predicts is absent. The dependency is inverted: you cannot map an operating envelope on top of an instrument that has no validated positive control and no working y-axis reference.
verdicts.feasibility_reality: Overstated as "medium / GPU + 3-4 weeks." The claimed asset is a single SHARED AMD MI300X ROCm droplet with no scheduler and other tenants on the same VRAM (per GPU_BOX_ACCESS_HANDOFF.md) — not a cluster. A 5-axis grid (SNR x depth x pert-strength x n-cells x gene-set) even at 4 levels = ~1024 cells x ~5 backends. The killer is seeds: wall1 shows retest support_jaccard 0.353 / edge_flip 0.482 at realistic depth, and the effects being resolved are <0.02 F1. To put a per-cell CI below the 0.02 gap that defines a phase boundary, you need ~20+ seeds per cell against 48% edge churn — i.e. ~100k inspre/GIES fits, with inspre-at-1000 being the expensive end. On one contended ROCm GPU (and most causal-discovery backends are CPU-bound anyway on a 20-CPU box) a properly powered sweep is weeks-to-months, not 3-4 weeks. Under-power it and every boundary you draw is sampling noise. So feasibility for a CREDIBLE diagram is low; feasibility for a noise-contour diagram is medium but worthless.
verdicts.scoop_risk: Moderate, not acute. arXiv 2605.04930 is the nearest work and explicitly leaves "link recovery failure to real-data properties" as future work, so the specific gap is genuinely open and the authors could extend to real data first — the pivot flags this correctly. But a defensive phase-diagram reframe of a benchmark null is not the kind of headline result a lab races to publish; it is a repositioning of an existing negative. The real scoop exposure is on the underlying substitutability claim, not on the phase-diagram framing per se. Named-lab race probability on THIS exact deliverable: low-to-moderate (~20-30%). Speed matters less than the pivot claims because the deliverable is unlikely to be a contested land-grab.
hidden_failure_modes: Empty-region collapse (most likely): real Perturb-seq is at/below the permutation null at EVERY scale already run (0.0346 vs null 0.0405 at N=500; below null at N=1000/1500). Sweeping SNR/depth is unlikely to open a region where real clears null, because the deficit is not obviously SNR-limited. Outcome: a phase diagram that is uniformly 'not identifiable' — the same null, re-plotted.

Does NOT escape the invariance/mean-only channel. The pivot is not a covariance training fix, so the invariance lemma does not kill it the SAME way it kills J2 (covariance_regularizer already exists in the repo, results/phase4/j2_covariance_regularizer.json — evidence a covariance fix was tried and could not register under mean-overlay eval). But the eval channel is still mean-only and the overlay is confounded, so the diagram's 'VCM tracks real' axis measures the mean channel plus the resampler, not the predictor's joint structure. It plots the broken instrument over a grid rather than escaping it.

Overlay-confound poisons the y-axis. overlay_resampled_real OUTSCORES the real cells it is derived from (0.0559 > 0.0346 at N=500). Any phase-diagram condition built on the overlay is partly measuring the resampler's covariance template, so 'where VCM tracks real' is not a clean measurement at any grid point — the confound is scale-invariant and rides through the whole sweep.

Construct-validity mismatch is invariant to the sweep. The anchored GT is a total-effect/reachability set while backends estimate direct edges, and correlational GRNBoost2 (0.0664) beats every causal backend. Sweeping SNR cannot fix a wrong-graph-type GT; the diagram inherits a y-axis that rewards correlation, so 'identifiable' cells may be identifying co-expression, not causal structure.

Boundaries below the noise floor. Per-cell contrasts are <0.02 F1 while test-retest reliability is 0.35 Jaccard / 0.48 edge-flip. Without heavy seeding the phase-boundary contours are indistinguishable from resampling noise; the elastic_net F1 swinging from 0.0327 (n=200) to 0.0003 (genome scale) shows how regime-sensitive and brittle these numbers are.

Reviewer reversal: a hostile ICML/Nature-methods reviewer reads 'operating envelope for causal use of VCM data' and asks for the positive-control cell; when none exists, the reframe reads as spin on a Type-II-limited null — potentially WORSE for credibility than shipping the honest bounded negative.

Compute-honesty trap on a shared box: a partially-run sweep (few seeds, coarse grid) invites 'you under-powered the very map that is your contribution,' which is unanswerable without the full run the timeline cannot afford.

verdicts.pivot: Positive method: covariance-regularized objective evaluated in the escaped (cell-output) channel
verdicts.verdict: Technically escapes the lemma, but not dead-on-arrival in the way the lemma predicts — it is dead-on-arrival for a worse reason. The Wall-1 lemma is a property of the mean-overlay channel only, so re-scoring cov_reg on predicted CELLS with covariance intact does formally leave the invariance channel; the penalty CAN register there. But the pivot trades a provably-blind channel for an empirically-null, low-reliability one. The project's own data already tests the one realized cell channel (real cells fed direct) and it scores at or below the permutation null at every scale (real_above_null=false). The A3 cell-channel win the pivot leans on is a +0.021 F1 contrast at n=5, sitting on the permutation floor and INSIDE the instrument's own 0.35-Jaccard / 48%-edge-flip test-retest band. So both branches collapse to the same root cause: at this SNR the cell channel cannot resolve a cov_reg effect. A 'positive' result would be a ~0.02 effect inside the noise band (not a credible method claim); a 'negative' result cannot claim adequate power because MDE > most effect magnitudes and the instrument fails its own positive control. This is not method-building — it is relocating an unidentifiable problem into a channel that needs its own validity fix first. Fix the instrument (depth, seeds, a causal-not-correlational GT, a working positive control) BEFORE any method pivot; otherwise this is high-effort busywork gated on an unbuilt, already-null escape channel.
hidden_failure_modes: Gating cascade: this pivot is gated on the escape-the-lemma pivot landing first, and the ONLY realized instance of that cell channel (real cells direct) is null vs permutation at every scale (results/instrument_fix/genome_scale_precision.json real_above_null=false). You would be building a method on top of a channel the project's own data shows is null.

Noise-band swamping: the cov_reg effect you hope to detect lives at the 0.001-0.02 F1 scale, but the cell-channel instrument has test-retest support_jaccard 0.353 and 48% edge-flip at realistic depth (wall1_invariance part2). A measurement with 0.35 retest reliability cannot rank a <0.02 method delta — a 'positive' result is indistinguishable from resampling churn.

Power fiction on the negative branch: claiming 'strong non-tautological negative with adequate power' is not supportable — MDE at n=5 is 0.0047 while effects of interest are smaller, and the benchmark has no passing positive control (real Perturb-seq itself sits at/below the permutation null). A null in the cell channel cannot be distinguished from 'the whole instrument recovers nothing.'

Mode-collapse ceiling (pivot's own admitted risk): even with a fair channel, VCMs must EMIT cells whose covariance is learnable. CPA inter-perturbation cosine is 0.845 vs real 0.084; STATE does not replicate across seeds. The predicted cell covariance is homogenized, so cov_reg has no usable joint structure to shape at inference.

GPU/stack feasibility: the definitive test needs end-to-end CPA/GEARS retraining with the Frobenius term, but the only compute is a single SHARED AMD MI300X on ROCm (GPU_BOX_ACCESS_HANDOFF.md), not CUDA. CPA/GEARS/scvi-tools are CUDA-first; getting NB-loss + custom covariance-penalty retraining green on ROCm shared VRAM, solo, in 3-4 weeks is optimistic — real risk the experiment never runs to completion.

Construct-validity contamination carries into the cell channel: the anchored GT rewards correlation (GRNBoost2 F1 0.066 outscores every causal backend incl. real-GIES). If cov_reg 'improves' cell-channel F1, it may be improving co-expression match, not causal recovery — the same artifact that already deflates every method, now dressed up as a positive method result.

Overlay-confound leakage: if any part of the 'cell channel' still routes through the resampler/overlay pool (which currently OUTPERFORMS real cells, D_overlay_pred 0.0231 > A_real 0.0164), a cov_reg 'gain' could be the resampler template injecting covariance, not the regularizer.
verdicts.feasibility_reality: Low, and lower than the card's own 'low' rating. Three compounding blockers: (1) gated on an unbuilt escape-channel pivot whose only realized form is empirically null; (2) requires end-to-end CPA/GEARS retraining on a single shared AMD MI300X/ROCm box while the perturbation-modeling stack (CPA/GEARS/scvi) is CUDA-oriented — a nontrivial port for a solo high-schooler on a 3-4 week timeline; (3) even if built, the target effect (0.001-0.02 F1) is below the cell-channel instrument's 0.35-Jaccard test-retest reliability and its MDE, so neither a positive nor an adequately-powered negative is obtainable without first re-engineering the instrument (depth, seed count, causal GT, working positive control) — work not in this pivot's scope. The existing covariance_regularizer.py + A3 anchored code + inspre-at-1000 assets are real, but they plug into a channel that cannot currently resolve the effect.
verdicts.scoop_risk: Low on this specific pivot; moderate on the umbrella. No named lab is racing to publish 'covariance-regularized VCM training scored in a cell-level causal channel' — it is a negative-leaning method footnote, not a flagship result. The broader 'VCMs fail at downstream/causal tasks' space IS crowded and moving fast (Ahlmann-Eltze/Huber linear-baseline critique, Arc Institute STATE, Theis/Lotfollahi CPA line, PerturBench, Mejia 2025 mode-collapse), so the benchmark framing itself has real scoop exposure — but the odds that a major lab independently ships THIS exact covariance-penalty-in-cell-channel experiment before a solo student are low. The risk is not being scooped; it is spending 3-4 GPU weeks producing a result too noisy to defend.

verdicts.feasibility_reality: Feasible on the solo/HS timeline and repo assets. All dependencies exist and are real: scripts/a1_perturbation_anchored_gt.py, GIES/inspre backends, genome_scale_precision.json rescore harness, GPU box for sweeps. The work is a re-scoring sweep over depth/pool/perturbation-strength strata plus a permutation null — days-to-1-week is credible because no new model training is required (it reuses cached predictions and the anchored-GT scorer). This is the cheapest high-leverage move available. The honest expected outcome, however, is a coin-flip: real_above_null is currently FALSE at every scale run, so there is a material chance no restricted window clears the null, which the pivot itself concedes forces a reframe rather than a fix.
verdicts.scoop_risk: Low on the pivot itself. A positive control / power analysis is table-stakes rigor, not a scoopable contribution — no lab publishes "we added a positive control." The BROADER negative thesis (VCMs cannot substitute for real Perturb-seq in causal discovery) carries moderate scoop exposure from Uhler/Squires (causal discovery from interventional genomics, Broad/MIT), Genentech PerturBench, and Theis/Lotfollahi (CPA/GEARS authors who could self-critique). But adding this control does not increase that exposure; if anything it hardens the paper against being scooped by a more rigorous version of the same critique.
hidden_failure_modes: INVERSION, not just null-failure: the pivot frames the risk as 'real may not clear null.' The sharper risk is in the data already — at K562 anchored GT, VCM conditions (cpa 0.0359, gears 0.0358) score ABOVE real_data (0.0314), and overlay (0.0559) beats real (0.0346) at genome scale. If the restricted window makes real clear the null, there is no guarantee VCM does not clear it too, equally or higher. That does not merely fail to support 'real > VCM' — it INVERTS the headline, and the pivot has no plan for that branch.

Construct-validity is orthogonal to SNR and survives the fix: the GT is a perturbation-DE reachability set (total-effect/ancestral) while GIES/inspre estimate direct edges, and correlational GRNBoost2 (0.0664) already beats every causal backend. Restricting to strong perturbations and raising depth cannot convert a total-effect GT into a direct-edge GT. A positive control that 'passes' in the high-SNR window may pass as a correlation detector, not as a causal-discovery validator — a working positive control for the WRONG estimand.

Test-retest reliability (0.353 Jaccard, 0.482 edge-flip at depth 1.0) sets an MDE that likely still exceeds the real-vs-VCM gap even inside the window. The pivot's 'pool cells / raise depth' lever does attack this (higher effective depth lowers Poisson churn), but pooling trades sampling noise for a smaller N of independent units and can inflate estimation variance — the reliability floor may not drop enough to rank methods separated by <0.02 F1.

Overlay-confound contamination of the control: overlay > real in the very numbers used to build the window (overlay 0.0329 vs real 0.0314 at K562; 0.0559 vs 0.0346 at genome scale). If the positive control is scored through any overlay/resampling arm, the resampler's covariance template — not the biology — supplies the signal. The control must be established on real cells directly AND must additionally demonstrate real > overlay inside the window, or it is measuring the instrument, not recovery.

Single-seed genome-scale evidence (seeds:[42]) means the current 'real clears/does not clear null' reads are themselves within one draw of the 0.353-Jaccard churn band; the window search must be multi-seed or it will chase sampling artifacts.

Multiple-comparison / garden-of-forking-paths: the pivot searches over perturbation-strength cut, coverage cut, depth, pooling, AND estimator-GT pairing simultaneously to find a window where real clears null. Without pre-registering the strata and null before looking, 'the one pairing where real_above_null=true margin > MDE' is a p-hacked survivor. This needs a locked null and a corrected threshold, or a hostile reviewer voids it instantly.
verdicts.verdict: Holds up and is correctly the do-first item — it is NOT busywork and it is NOT dead-on-arrival to the invariance lemma. The Wall-1 lemma says a covariance TRAINING fix cannot register under mean-overlay evaluation; this pivot makes no such fix and does not need covariance to register. It operates on a different axis entirely — whether the evaluation instrument has ANY discriminating power in ANY regime — so it cleanly escapes the lemma channel. That is its main virtue. But its impact is DEFENSIVE (+5 survival, not +5 growth): it prevents the negative thesis from being uninterpretable, it does not add novelty (self-assessed +1 is honest). Three caveats keep it from being a clean win: (1) it fixes only the positive-control weakness, leaving the construct-validity mismatch (correlation beats causal against this GT) and the overlay confound untouched — both survive any SNR restriction; (2) the raw numbers already show VCM >= real inside the noise band, so the likely branches are 'no window clears null' (thesis becomes unidentifiability, a reframe) or 'window clears but VCM clears too' (thesis inverts) — the pivot only plans for the first; (3) the window search is a forking-paths engine and must pre-register strata + lock the permutation null + go multi-seed before looking, or it self-invalidates. Do it first, but run it as a pre-registered, real-cell-only, multi-seed test with the inversion and equality branches specified in advance — and treat 'task unidentifiable at this SNR' as the modal, publishable-if-honest outcome, not a failure.

verdicts.pivot: Match the graph object (estimand-GT alignment + explicit correlational baseline)
verdicts.feasibility_reality: Feasible but the two arms are not equal. Path (a) — total-effect/ACE ranking against the reachability GT — is the cheap, tractable one: the anchored-GT builder (scripts/a1_perturbation_anchored_gt.py), GRNBoost2 baseline, and inspre-at-1000 scaling all already exist, GPU box access is live per the handoff, and linear-SEM ACE ranking is a 1-2 week job. That arm's 1-2 week estimate is honest. Path (b) — building a direct-edge GT via single-hop CRISPRi + mediation pruning — is under-scoped and possibly ill-posed for a solo HS timeline: (i) transitive REDUCTION is not unique or even well-defined on a cyclic GRN, and mediation pruning assumes a DAG the network isn't; (ii) there is no clean single-hop measurement to build the GT from — a DE effect at the Perturb-seq readout timepoint already contains multi-hop propagation, so the 'direct-edge GT' inherits the same total-effect contamination it is meant to remove. Recommend committing to path (a) only and treating (b) as out of scope.
verdicts.scoop_risk: Moderate, and it undercuts the impact_delta of 4. The core empirical finding this pivot chases — correlational co-expression matching or beating causal-discovery backends on Perturb-seq GRN recovery — is ALREADY PUBLISHED by CausalBench (Chevalley et al., Mila/GSK) and the 2023 CausalBench Challenge, which the pivot's own risk field cites. So arm (a) confirming 'correlational ties causal on a properly-matched GT' is a replication, not a discovery. No named lab is racing the specific estimand-GT-alignment-for-VCM-substitutability framing as-is, but that framing is the ONLY differentiator left, and it is fragile. Adjacent active groups (Theis, Lopez/Regev on causal representation, the GEARS/scGPT perturbation-modeling crowd) have all the building blocks. Net: the defensible novelty is the benchmark-hygiene contribution, not the empirical result.
hidden_failure_modes: Does NOT escape the fatal blocker. The critical weakness is the missing positive control: real Perturb-seq scores at or below the permutation null at every scale (N=1000: 0.0068 vs null 0.0201, real_above_null=false everywhere). Matching the graph object is the single most plausible route to lifting real above null — but it is a gamble, not a fix. If ACE-vs-reachability still leaves real at the null (very possible, since low SNR, not graph-type mismatch, may be the true cause), the pivot has spent 1-2 weeks to CONFIRM the task is unidentifiable and guts the headline substitutability claim. The pivot's own risk field concedes exactly this.

Escapes the invariance-lemma channel but leaves the test-retest reliability floor untouched. The pivot correctly operates on a different axis than the mean-overlay covariance-blindness lemma (it changes the GT/estimand, not the overlay), so it is NOT dead-on-arrival through that specific channel. BUT wall1_invariance.json shows support_jaccard 0.353 / 48% edge-flip at realistic depth. Total-effect/ACE edge recovery is just as exposed to Poisson-resampling churn as direct-edge recovery. Every contrast in the paper is a <0.02 F1 difference sitting inside a ~0.35-Jaccard noise band — matching the graph object does nothing to widen that gap, so the ranking claims remain unsupportable at the depth the benchmark runs regardless of this pivot.

Leaves the overlay-confound (overlay outperforms the real cells it is derived from: 0.0559 vs 0.0346 at N=500) completely unaddressed. Any conclusion that still routes through overlay conditions inherits the resampler-injects-covariance artifact.

Path (b) premise failure: 'single-hop CRISPRi effects, mediation-pruned' assumes a DAG and a clean one-hop readout that this cyclic-network, propagated-timepoint data cannot supply; the constructed direct-edge GT would re-import total-effect contamination, silently reproducing the very mismatch it claims to remove.

ACE ranking under a linear-Gaussian SEM on overdispersed count data is model-misspecified; a 'matched' result could be an artifact of the estimator's parametric assumptions rather than genuine recovery.

Novelty_delta +2 is optimistic given CausalBench priority — a hostile reviewer reads arm (a) as replicating a known negative result under a relabeled GT, so the contribution collapses to construct-validity hygiene unless the VCM-substitutability framing is doing heavy independent lifting.
verdicts.verdict: Not busywork, but not a rescue either — it is the correct, highest-leverage DIAGNOSTIC to run next, sequenced ahead of everything else, precisely because it is the only listed move that could restore a working positive control, and without one the entire substitutability thesis is Type-II-limited and unpublishable. It genuinely escapes the mean-overlay invariance lemma (different axis: GT/estimand, not overlay covariance-blindness), so it is not dead on arrival through that channel. Do it, but scope it to path (a) only (drop the ill-posed direct-edge-GT arm), report the correlational baseline explicitly, and pre-register the outcome BOTH ways: if matched ACE-vs-reachability lifts real above null and separates from correlational, you have your instrument; if it does not, you have honestly established the task is unidentifiable at this SNR — reframe the paper around that as the finding rather than forcing the VCM-inadequacy narrative. Two caveats keep this at priority 4 not 5: (1) it does not touch the two other critical/high blockers (0.35 test-retest Jaccard, overlay-outperforms-real confound), so it is necessary-but-not-sufficient; (2) the empirical result it targets is largely pre-scooped by CausalBench, so the durable contribution is benchmark validity, not a new finding.
